page: Amazon Web Services r5n.16xlarge is a memory-optimized and network-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores based on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage.

In benchmark evaluations, the r5n.16xlarge demonstrates strong memory performance, particularly in large-block read and write operations, which rank in the top 25 percent. Passmark database operations and extended instruction tests also score in the top 25 percent. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by stress-ng single-core and single-threaded compression benchmarks. Multi-core performance and software compilation benchmarks fall into the average range.

This instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads that require significant network throughput, offering a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps. The high memory capacity and strong database benchmark results make it highly suitable for large-scale databases, in-memory caches, and data-intensive processing tasks. The lack of local storage requires reliance on EBS, but the high network bandwidth mitigates potential storage bottlenecks. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high resource density of memory and network bandwidth provides a balanced profile for enterprise-scale deployments without the overhead of local NVMe drives.
description: Amazon Web Services r5n.16xlarge is a memory-optimized and network-optimized instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz, paired with 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on network-attached storage supported by a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps. Benchmark data shows strong performance in large-block memory bandwidth and database operations, though single-core compute performance is relatively weak. This hardware profile makes the instance highly efficient for memory-intensive applications, in-memory databases, and high-throughput network workloads. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core ensures that large datasets can be cached and processed efficiently in memory, while the robust network interface supports demanding data transfer requirements.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5n.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR4 RAM, and 75 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong database and large-block memory performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r5n.16xlarge features 64 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR4 RAM, and 75 Gbps network bandwidth for memory-intensive database workloads.
tagline: This memory-optimized, high-bandwidth virtual machine is designed for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and data-intensive enterprise network workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 8.0 GB per core.
Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor.
75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfer.
Strong benchmark performance in large-block memory bandwidth and database operations.
EBS-only storage configuration with no local drives or GPU accelerators.
Best suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and data-intensive processing.
